title: Lost in the City
summary: A tourist asks a local for directions after getting lost on the way to the museum.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Excuse me! I am sorry to bother you. I think I am lost.

body.speaker: Local
body.text: No problem! Where do you want to go?

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: I am looking for the city museum. Do you know where it is?

body.speaker: Local
body.text: Yes, I do! The museum is not far from here. It is about ten minutes on foot.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Oh, that is good to hear. Can you tell me how to get there?

body.speaker: Local
body.text: Of course. Go straight ahead on this street. You will pass a big park on your left.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Okay, I go straight and there is a park on the left. Then what?

body.speaker: Local
body.text: Then turn right at the traffic lights. Walk for two more minutes.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Turn right at the traffic lights. Got it. Is the museum easy to find?

body.speaker: Local
body.text: Yes, it is a large grey building. There is a fountain in front of it.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: A large grey building with a fountain. That sounds easy. Thank you so much!

body.speaker: Local
body.text: You are welcome! Enjoy your visit to the museum.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: I will! One more question — is there a good café nearby?

body.speaker: Local
body.text: Yes! There is a small café just next to the museum. The coffee is very good there.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Perfect! You have been so helpful. Have a great day!

body.speaker: Local
body.text: You too! Enjoy your time in our city.

annotations.exampleEn: I am sorry to bother you, but can you help me?
It is only five minutes on foot from here.
Go straight ahead and you will see the school.
The supermarket is on your left.
Turn right at the corner to find the hotel.
Stop when the traffic lights are red.
There is a beautiful fountain in the town square.
The café is just next to the post office.

annotations.meaningVi: tận hưởng chuyến thăm của bạn
annotations.exampleEn: Enjoy your visit to the gallery!
The library is a large grey building on the main street.
Thank you! You have a great day too.

grammarNotes.title: Imperative for giving directions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dạng mệnh lệnh (imperative) dùng động từ nguyên mẫu để chỉ đường: 'Go straight ahead.' / 'Turn right at the traffic lights.' / 'Walk for two more minutes.' Không dùng chủ ngữ 'you' trước động từ trong câu mệnh lệnh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The imperative uses the base verb form to give directions: 'Go straight ahead.' / 'Turn right at the traffic lights.' / 'Walk for two more minutes.' There is no subject 'you' before the verb in imperative sentences.

grammarNotes.title: Prepositions of place
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Giới từ chỉ vị trí rất quan trọng khi chỉ đường: 'on your left' (bên trái), 'in front of' (phía trước), 'next to' (bên cạnh), 'on this street' (trên con đường này). Học thuộc các giới từ này để hỏi và chỉ đường dễ hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Prepositions of place are essential for directions: 'on your left,' 'in front of,' 'next to,' 'on this street.' Learning these prepositions helps you ask for and understand directions more easily.

translationVi: Du khách: Xin lỗi! Xin lỗi đã làm phiền bạn. Tôi nghĩ tôi bị lạc rồi. Người địa phương: Không sao! Bạn muốn đến đâu? Du khách: Tôi đang tìm bảo tàng thành phố. Bạn có biết ở đâu không? Người địa phương: Biết chứ! Bảo tàng không xa đây lắm. Đi bộ khoảng mười phút. Du khách: Ồ, nghe tốt quá. Bạn có thể chỉ đường cho tôi không? Người địa phương: Tất nhiên. Đi thẳng trên con phố này. Bạn sẽ đi qua một công viên lớn bên trái. Du khách: Được, đi thẳng và có công viên bên trái. Sau đó thì sao? Người địa phương: Sau đó rẽ phải ở đèn giao thông. Đi thêm hai phút. Du khách: Rẽ phải ở đèn giao thông. Hiểu rồi. Bảo tàng có dễ tìm không? Người địa phương: Có, đó là một tòa nhà lớn màu xám. Có đài phun nước phía trước. Du khách: Tòa nhà lớn màu xám với đài phun nước. Nghe có vẻ dễ. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều! Người địa phương: Không có gì! Chúc bạn tham quan bảo tàng vui vẻ. Du khách: Chắc chắn rồi! Còn một câu hỏi — có quán cà phê nào gần đây không? Người địa phương: Có! Có một quán cà phê nhỏ ngay bên cạnh bảo tàng. Cà phê ở đó rất ngon. Du khách: Tuyệt! Bạn đã giúp đỡ tôi rất nhiều. Chúc bạn một ngày tốt lành! Người địa phương: Bạn cũng vậy! Chúc bạn có thời gian vui vẻ tại thành phố của chúng tôi.

questions.prompt: Why does the tourist stop the local?
options: She is looking for a café

questions.prompt: Where does the tourist want to go?
questions.explanation: 'I am looking for the city museum.'
'It is about ten minutes on foot.'
questions.hint: Find the local's description of the distance.

questions.prompt: What will the tourist pass on her left?
questions.explanation: 'You will pass a big park on your left.'
questions.hint: Find the first landmark in the directions.

questions.prompt: Where does the tourist turn right?
questions.explanation: 'Then turn right at the traffic lights.'

questions.prompt: What colour is the museum building?
questions.hint: Find the description of the museum.

questions.prompt: What is in front of the museum?
questions.explanation: 'There is a fountain in front of it.'
questions.hint: Find the landmark near the museum entrance.

questions.prompt: What does the tourist ask about after getting directions?

options: Across the road from the museum
questions.explanation: 'There is a small café just next to the museum.'
questions.hint: Find the local's description of the café.

questions.prompt: How does the local describe the coffee at the café?
questions.hint: Find the local's comment about the café.

questions.prompt: How does the tourist feel at the end of the conversation?
questions.explanation: 'You have been so helpful. Have a great day!'
questions.hint: Look at the tourist's last two lines.

questions.prompt: How many more minutes should the tourist walk after turning right?
questions.hint: Find the walking time after the turn.

It is about ten minutes on {{b3}}. Tourist: Can you tell me how to get there? Local: Go {{b4}} ahead on this street. You will pass a big park on your {{b5}}. Tourist: Okay. Then what? Local: Then turn {{b6}} at the traffic lights. Walk for two more {{b7}}. Tourist: Is the museum easy to find? Local: Yes, it is a large {{b8}} building. There is a {{b9}} in front of it. Tourist: That sounds easy. Thank you! Local: You are welcome! Enjoy your {{b10}} to the museum. Tourist: Is there a good café {{b11}}? Local: Yes! There is a small café just {{b12}} to the museum. The coffee is very {{b13}} there.

blanks.explanation: 'I am looking for the city museum.'
'It is about ten minutes on foot.'
'You will pass a big park on your left.'
'Then turn right at the traffic lights.'
'There is a fountain in front of it.'
'a small café just next to the museum'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The tourist was lost and asked a stranger for help. Do you think it is easy to ask strangers for help? What other ways can a tourist find directions in a new city?
